zh.Aylesford, Kent, England: 英國英格蘭肯特艾勒斯福德
zh.Derby, Derbyshire, England: 英國英格蘭德比夏爾德比
zh.lifeSummary: 當Richard de Grey 2nd Baron de Gray於1281年出生在英國德比夏爾可德諾時，他的父親Henry de Grey 1st Baron25歲，母親Eleanor de Courtenay20歲。他於1302年之前與Joan Fitz Payne在英國英格蘭德比夏爾可德諾結婚。他們至少是3個兒子和1個女兒的父母。他於1325年被列為constable of nottingham castle。他去世於1335年3月10日在英國德比夏爾享年54歲，埋葬在英國德比夏爾可德諾。

zh-hans.lifeSummary: 当Richard de Grey 2nd Baron de Gray于1281年出生在英国德比夏爾可德諾时，他的父亲Henry de Grey 1st Baron25岁，母亲Eleanor de Courtenay20岁。他于1302年之前与Joan Fitz Payne在英国英格兰德比夏爾可德諾结婚。他们至少是3个儿子和1个女儿的父母。他于1325年被列为constable of nottingham castle。他去世于1335年3月10日在英国德比夏爾享年54岁，埋葬在英国德比夏爾可德諾。

nameMeanings.name: de
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Indian Dilip, Anirban, Gopal, Alok, Anindya, Arunava, Asit, Asok, Dibyendu, Goutam, Indra, Indranil.

variants.name: Dey
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Indian Ashit, Indu, Naina, Partha, Soumya, Ajit, Anjan, Anjana, Anup, Arjun, Arun, Ashim.
English, Scottish, and Welsh: variant of   Day  .
German: topographic name from a field name in North Rhine-Westphalia, denoting a sizeable piece of land, from Middle Low German dē ‘thigh’ (referring to the shape of the property).

nameMeanings.name: Grey
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: English, Scottish, and Irish: variant of   Gray  .
In some cases also an American shortened and altered form of Ukrainian Grushko: nickname or topographic name derived from a Russian-influenced transliteration of grushka ‘pear, pear tree’.

variants.name: Gray
meanings: English, Scottish, and Irish (especially Eastern Ulster; of Norman origin): habitational name from Graye in Calvados, France, named from the Gallo-Roman personal name Graec(i)us, meaning ‘Greek’ + the locative suffix -acum. This is probably the chief source of the surname in Britain.

English: nickname for someone with gray hair or a gray beard, from Middle English grey (Old English grǣg, grēg) ‘gray’. In Ireland it has been used as a translation of various Gaelic surnames derived from riabhach ‘brindled, gray’, including Mac Giolla Riabhaigh; see McGreevy . In North America, this surname has assimilated names with similar meaning from other languages.
French: habitational name from Gray in Haute-Saône or Le Gray in Seine-Maritime.

variants.name: Greiwe
meanings: German: possibly a nickname from Middle High German greibe ‘sharp, tangy, dour’. Compare Grey .

nameMeanings.name: Richard
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: One of the most enduringly successful of the Old French personal names introduced into Britain by the Normans. It is of Germanic (Frankish) origin, derived from rīc ‘power’ + hard ‘strong, hardy’. It has enjoyed continuous popularity in England from the Conquest to the present day, influenced by the fact that it was borne by three kings of England, in particular Richard I ( 1157–99 ). He was king for only ten years ( 1189–99 ), most of which he spent in warfare abroad, taking part in the Third Crusade and costing the people of England considerable sums in taxes. Nevertheless, he achieved the status of a folk hero, and was never in England long enough to disappoint popular faith in his goodness and justice. He was also Duke of Aquitaine and Normandy and Count of Anjou, fiefs which he held at a time of maximum English expansion in France. His exploits as a leader of the Third Crusade earned him the nickname ‘Coeur de Lion’ or ‘Lionheart’ and a permanent place in popular imagination, in which he was even more firmly enshrined by Sir Walter Scott's novel Ivanhoe ( 1820 ).

variants.name: Rikert

He was king for only ten years ( 1189–99 ), most of which he spent in warfare abroad, taking part in the Third Crusade and costing the people of England considerable sums in taxes. His exploits as a leader of the Third Crusade earned him the nickname ‘Coeur de Lion’ or ‘Lionheart’ and a permanent place in popular imagination, in which he was even more firmly enshrined by Sir Walter Scott's novel Ivanhoe ( 1820 ).

historicEvents.title: Jews Expelled From England
historicEvents.description: After years of anti-Semitic behavior, King Edward I expelled Jews from England in 1290.

historicEvents.title: Famine Strikes Europe
historicEvents.description: A famine caused by cold winters and wet summers across Europe caused millions to die due to starvation. Desperation from hunger caused high levels of crime, stampedes in bread lines in London, infanticide, and even cannibalism throughout Europe.

historicEvents.title: First War of Scottish Independence
historicEvents.description: The First War of Scottish Independence began after England invaded Scotland in 1296. The Treaty of Edinburgh was signed in 1328 which declared peace between the two kingdoms and brought the war to an end.

en.lifeSummary: When Richard de Grey 2nd Baron de Gray was born in 1281, in Codnor, Derbyshire, England, his father, Henry de Grey 1st Baron, was 25 and his mother, Eleanor de Courtenay, was 20. He married Joan Fitz Payne before 1302, in Codnor, Derbyshire, England, United Kingdom. They were the parents of at least 3 sons and 1 daughter. In 1325, his occupation is listed as constable of nottingham castle. He died on 10 March 1335, in Derbyshire, England, at the age of 54, and was buried in Codnor, Derbyshire, England.

fr.lifeSummary: Lorsque Richard de Grey 2nd Baron de Gray est né en 1281, à Codnor, Derbyshire, Angleterre, son père, Henry de Grey 1st Baron, avait 25 ans et sa mère, Eleanor de Courtenay, avait 20 ans. Il a épousé Joan Fitz Payne avant 1302, à Codnor, Derbyshire, Angleterre, Royaume-Uni. Ils ont eu au moins 3 fils et 1 fille. En 1325, sa profession de son indiquée est constable of nottingham castle. Il est décédé le 10 mars 1335, à Derbyshire, Angleterre, à l’âge de 54 ans, et a été enterré à Codnor, Derbyshire, Angleterre.

de.lifeSummary: Als Richard de Grey 2nd Baron de Gray 1281 in Codnor, Derbyshire, England geboren wurde, war sein Vater Henry de Grey 1st Baron 25 und seine Mutter Eleanor de Courtenay 20. Er hat vor 1302 in Codnor, Derbyshire, England, United Kingdom Joan Fitz Payne geheiratet. Sie hatten mindestens 3 Söhne und 1 Tochter. 1325 ist sein Beruf als constable of nottingham castle aufgeführt. Er starb am 10. März 1335 im Alter von 54 Jahren in Derbyshire, England und wurde in Codnor, Derbyshire, England begraben.

it.lifeSummary: Quando Richard de Grey 2nd Baron de Gray è nato il nel 1281 nel Codnor, Derbyshire, England, il suo padre, Henry de Grey 1st Baron, aveva 25 anni e il suo madre, Eleanor de Courtenay, aveva 20 anni. Lui sposato Joan Fitz Payne prima del 1302, nel Codnor, Derbyshire, Inghilterra, Regno Unito. Erano genitori di almeno 3 figli e 1 figlia. Nel 1325, la sua occupazione è indicata come constable of nottingham castle. Morì il 10 marzo 1335, nel Derbyshire, England, all'età di 54 anni, e fu sepolto a Codnor, Derbyshire, England.

ja.lifeSummary: Richard de Grey 2nd Baron de Grayが1281年にイギリスデルブイシュアイアコドノーで生まれた時，父Henry de Grey 1st Baronは25歳，母Eleanor de Courtenayは20歳であった。1302年以前にイギリスイギリスデルブイシュアイアコドノーでJoan Fitz Payneと結婚した。彼らは少なくとも3人の息子と1人の娘の親であった。1325年，職業はconstable of nottingham castleと記録されている。彼は1335年3月10日にイギリスデルブイシュアイアで54歳で死去し，イギリスデルブイシュアイアコドノーにて埋葬された。

ko.lifeSummary: Richard de Grey 2nd Baron de Gray(이)가 1281년 영국 더비실르 코드너에서 태어났을 때, 그의 아버지 Henry de Grey 1st Baron(은)는 25세였고, 그의 어머니 Eleanor de Courtenay(은)는 20세였다. 그는 1302년 이전 영국 잉글랜드 더비실르 코드너에서 Joan Fitz Payne(와)과 결혼했다. 그들은 적어도 세 명의 아들과 한 명의 딸을 두었다. 1325년, 그의 직업은 constable of nottingham castle이었다. 그는 1335년 3월 10일 영국 더비실르에서 54세의 나이로 사망했으며 영국 더비실르 코드너에 묻혔다.

pt.lifeSummary: Quando Richard de Grey 2nd Baron de Gray nasceu em 1281, em Codnor, Derbyshire, Inglaterra, seu pai, Henry de Grey 1st Baron, tinha 25 anos e sua mãe, Eleanor de Courtenay, tinha 20 anos. Ele casou-se com Joan Fitz Payne antes 1302, em Codnor, Derbyshire, Inglaterra, United Kingdom. Eles tiveram pelo menos 3 filhos e 1 filha. Em 1325, sua profissão aparece como constable of nottingham castle. Ele faleceu em 10 de março de 1335, em Derbyshire, Inglaterra, com 54 anos, e foi sepultado em Codnor, Derbyshire, Inglaterra.

ru.lifeSummary: Когда Richard de Grey 2nd Baron de Gray родился в 1281 г. в местности коднор, дирбишир, Англия, его отцу по имени Henry de Grey 1st Baron было 25 лет, а матери, которую звали Eleanor de Courtenay, 20. Он женился на женщине по имени Joan Fitz Payne перед 1302 г. в местности коднор, дирбишир, Англия, Великобритания. У них было по крайней мере 3 сына и 1 дочь. В 1325 году он работал по профессии constable of nottingham castle. Он умер 10 марта 1335 г. в местности дирбишир, Англия в возрасте 54 лет и был похоронен в местности коднор, дирбишир, Англия.

es.lifeSummary: Cuando Richard de Grey 2nd Baron de Gray nació en 1281, en Codnor, Derbyshire, Inglaterra, su papá, Henry de Grey 1st Baron, tenía 25 años y su mamá, Eleanor de Courtenay, tenía 20 años. Se casó con Joan Fitz Payne antes de 1302, en Codnor, Derbyshire, Inglaterra, Reino Unido. Fueron padres de por lo menos 3 hijos y 1 hija. En 1325, su ocupación registrada era constable of nottingham castle. Murió el 10 de marzo de 1335, en Derbyshire, Inglaterra, a la edad de 54 años, y fue sepultado en Codnor, Derbyshire, Inglaterra.
